paper_title: Environmental Sampling and Analysis of Marine Sediments

methods.description: Process for collecting marine sediment samples and isolating bacteria for further analysis.

steps.description: A total of 49 marine sediment samples were collected around the British Columbia coast and fjord from 2007 to 2009.
steps.searchable_summary: collect marine sediment samples from specified locations

steps.description: Sediments from depths of 20 to 60 m were collected by scuba diving, while sites at a depth of 80 to 200 m were obtained by a sand grabber, and the samples were placed in a sterile 50 mL conical tubes.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples using scuba diving and sand grabber

steps.description: Samples were kept at 4°C during sampling and were processed as soon as possible after collection by desiccation and heat shock method.
steps.searchable_summary: store samples at 4°C and process using desiccation and heat shock

steps.description: Samples were inoculated in marine media prepared with 100% natural sea water or normal saline solution and amended with filtered cycloheximide and rifamycin, after autoclaving.
steps.searchable_summary: inoculate samples in marine media with antibiotics

steps.description: Actinomycetes growing on the isolation plates were transferred with sterile inoculating loops to MM1 medium until they are pure.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer actinomycetes to MM1 medium for purification

steps.description: Pure cultures were stored frozen in MM1 broth with 20% glycerol at -80°C for long-term storage.
steps.searchable_summary: store pure cultures in MM1 broth with glycerol at -80°C

methods.method_name: Amplification, Sequencing, and Analysis of 16S rRNA Gene
methods.description: Procedure for amplifying, sequencing, and analyzing the 16S rRNA gene from isolated bacterial cultures.

steps.description: The total DNA was isolated using a DNeasy blood and tissue kit by picking a single of the pure cultured isolates according to the manufacturers instruction.
Primers F27 and RC_1492 were used to amplify 1490 bp of the 16S rRNA gene.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify 16S rRNA gene using specific primers

steps.description: The PCR conditions were as follows: initial denaturation at 98°C for 3 min; 35 cycles at 98°C for 10 s, 60°C for 10s and 72°C for 60 s.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR with specified thermal cycling conditions

steps.description: The amplification products were cleaned up by QIAquick PCR cleanup kit according to manufacturer's protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: clean up PCR products using QIAquick kit

steps.description: The nearly complete 16S rRNA gene was sequenced by using the PCR products directly as sequencing templates with specific primers.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence 16S rRNA gene using PCR products as templates

steps.description: Comparison of the 16S rRNA gene sequences of the isolated actinomycete was determined by Basic Local Alignment Search Tool (nucleotide blast) similarity searching GenBank nucleotide database.
The 16S rRNA gene sequences of the related reference strains and other isolates were obtained from GenBank and aligned with ClustalX.

The phylogenetic tree based on the highly variable γ-region of the 16S rRNA gene sequence to classify representatives and resolve relationship within the genus Streptomyces was used to construct a multiple alignment with the neighboring-joining method and a matrix of Jukes-Cantor distances provided with the Mega5 software using 2,000 bootstrap replicates.

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing chemical compounds in culture extracts using chromatography and spectroscopy techniques.

steps.description: The active isolates were cultured on 5 pans of solid agar, equivalent to 2 L volume of the marine medium 1 at RT for 14 days.
steps.searchable_summary: culture isolates on solid agar for 14 days

steps.description: The mature cultures were sliced into small squares containing the bacterial biomass and the media, and extracted twice with EtOAc.
The combined EtOAc extracts were concentrated in vacuo and partitioned between H2O and EtOAc.
The EtOAc soluble material was chromatographed on Sephadex LH20 with 4:1 methanol/CH2Cl2 as eluent.
The fractions were further purified to preparative reversed-phase HPLC to isolate the active pure compound.
UV spectra were recorded with a Waters 996 Photodiode Array Detector.
The 1H and 13C NMR spectra were recorded on a Bruker AV-600 spectrometer with a 5 mm CPTCI cryoprobe.
Low and high resolution ESIQIT-MS were recorded on a Bruker-Hewlett Packard 1100 Esquire-LC system mass spectrometer.
The resulting chemical data were compared with data from Antibase, Sci-Finder and MarinLit.
